name: experience-mapping-skills
description: A flat collection of ten codebase-neutral skills for mapping the experience of a multi-surface developer tool (CLI, SDK, REST API, MCP server, desktop/Electron dashboard, installer/upgrade, docs). Generative, not critique — each skill produces one structured artifact. Ships experience-map (product-agnostic baseline + white space), journey-map (one actor across the adoption funnel), service-blueprint (frontstage/backstage root-cause), current-future-state (as-is vs to-be + gap backlog), jtbd-map (jobs / four forces / desired outcomes), touchpoint-inventory (every surface x task x owner, incl. non-GUI touchpoints), ecosystem-map (the system graph + broken loops), concept-terminology-audit (cross-surface naming drift, partly machine-checkable), surface-information-architecture (each surface as a projection of one concept model), and cross-surface-synthesis (the master JTBD x surface matrix). No agents, no shared docs — each skill stands alone and auto-routes on its own triggers. Pairs with dx-evaluation-skills: map first, then evaluate.
